description_long: This bungalow features a broad, low-pitched roof with wide overhangs, characteristic of early 20th-century American residential architecture. The structure is single-storied, with a rectangular footprint and a prominent, horizontally oriented roofline. The roof extends well beyond the main walls, suggesting deep eaves that provide shade and architectural interest. The silhouette hints at a possible front porch area, though details are minimal, and the overall massing is compact and grounded. The exterior would likely be clad in wood or brick, with windows arranged to maximize natural light and ventilation. The design is practical and inviting, suitable for suburban or neighborhood settings.
description: A single-story bungalow with a wide, low-pitched roof and deep overhangs, featuring a compact, rectangular form and a possible front porch area.
description_view_0: Front view showing the wide, overhanging roof and the main entrance facade, with a clear, symmetrical outline.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the extended roof eaves and the elongated profile of the bungalow.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the back elevation and the full width of the roof overhangs.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, similar to view 1, emphasizing the depth of the roof and the side wall.
